observations.recommendation: 1) In view of the foregoing, we recommend the Local Chief Executive to instruct the Accounting and Supply Management Office to coordinate in the implementation of the Perpetual Inventory method of recording Inventories. In addition to this, it should be ascertained that necessary ledger cards are kept by the Accounting Office for each inventory, and submission of the Report on the Physical Count of Inventory (RPCI) by the Supply Management Office must be religiously complied with so the former may make necessary actions or adjustments, if any.

In line with this, we recommend the Local Chief Executive to: 2) Require the supply/property inspector to conduct pre-repair inspection and post-repair inspection to the properties subjected to repairs and furnish the reports to the COA Auditor within five (5) days from the date of such evaluation/inspection; 3) See to it that a Waste Management Report is prepared when it involves replacement of worn-out spare parts; 4) Direct the General Services Officer to maintain records of the history of repairs of each LGU property, plant and equipment.

9) To create an inventory committee to conduct the physical count of its properties, plant equipment, and shall be made annually and reported on the Report on the Physical Count of Property, Plant and Equipment (RPCPPE), in accordance with Section 156 of the Commission on Audit (COA) Circular No. 92-386 dated October 20, 1992. 10) The reconciliation of the record of the GSO and the Municipal Accountant to up-date the property ledger card to reflect the true and correct valuation of the said property and submit the same to the Local Auditor concerned not later than January 31, of each year, pursuant to Sections 119, 120 and 124 of the Manual New Government Accounting System (MNGAS) by Local Government Unit, Volume I.

12) Abide Section V.1, V.2, V.3, V.8 and V.9 of Commission On Audit Circular 2015-008 dated November 23, 2015. 13) Create an inventory committee of the municipality to conduct the physical count of its Road Network and prepare a report thereon (see Annex C); 14) Require the Municipal Accountant to update the local Road Network account based on the result of the physical count and disclose the said account on the Notes to Financial Statements (see Annex B); 15) Direct the General Services Officer to maintain a Local Road inventory and Road Map and prepare a report thereon at the end of every accounting period (See Annex A) as well as keep a complete Local Road Network Property Card for all roads and its components (see Annex E). 16) Require the Municipal Engineer to provide the accountant and the General Services Officer with the complete description and cost segregation of road components for road projects.

1.6 We recommended that the LCE:
a) Create an inventory committee to conduct the physical count of its PPE
which shall be made annually and reported on the RPCPPE, in accordance
with Section 156 of COA Circular No. 92-386 dated October 20, 1992; and
b) Require the General Service Office and the Municipal Accountant to
reconcile and up-date the property ledger card to reflect the true and
correct valuation of the said property and submit the same to the Auditor
concerned not later than January 31, of each year, pursuant to Sections
119,120 and 124 of the MNGAS by LGU Volume I.

2.5 In relation to this, we recommended the LCE:
a) Create an inventory committee of the municipality to conduct the physical
count of its Road Network and prepare a report thereon.
b) Require the Municipal Accountant to update the local Road Network
account based on the result of the physical count and disclose the said
account on the Notes to Financial Statements.
c) Direct the General Services Officer to maintain a Local Road inventory
and Road Map and prepare a report thereon at the end of every
accounting period as well as keep a complete Local Road Network
Property Card for all roads and its components.
d) Require the Municipal Engineer to provide the accountant and the
General Services Officer with the complete description and cost
segregation of road components for road projects.

3.6 Thus, we reiterated our recommendations that Management:
a) Create an inventory committee to conduct physical inventory of all PPEs
of the Municipality, be it acquired through purchase or donation,
including those constructed by administration found at station, pursuant
to COA Circular No. 2020-006 dated January 31, 2020.
b) Instruct the Supply and Property Unit and Accounting Office to
strengthen its system of maintaining its accounting and property records
such as PCs, PPELCs, and RPCPPE to facilitate periodic reconciliation of
these records to ensure reliability of the PPE account balances as
presented in the Financial Statements.
d) Undertake all other procedures prescribed under COA Circular 2020-006
dated January 31, 2020, to come up with the reconciled and correct
balances of PPE accounts in the financial statements; and
e) After inventory taking, direct the Municipal Accountant to prepare the
updated ledger cards for each item of PPE pursuant to the Section 13 of
the MNGAS by LGU Volume I.

4.6 We recommended that the LCE direct the municipal accountant to faithfully
enforce the submission of report and supporting documents to the auditor
within first ten (10) days of ensuing month in compliance of Section 7 (7.2.1)
of the RRSA and MNGAS for LGUs V. I.

5.5 In view of the foregoing, we recommended that LCE instruct the Accounting
and Supply Management Office to coordinate in the implementation of the
Perpetual Inventory method of recording Inventories. In addition to this, it
should be ascertained that necessary ledger cards are kept by the Accounting
Office for each inventory, and submission of the Report on the Physical Count
of Inventory (RPCI) by the Supply Management Office must be religiously
complied with so the former may make necessary actions or adjustments, if any.

6.5 In line with this, we reiterated our recommendation that the LCE:
a) Require the supply/property inspector to conduct pre-repair inspection
and post-repair inspection to the properties subjected to repairs and
furnish the reports to the COA Auditor within five (5) days from the date
of such evaluation/inspection.
b) See to it that a Waste Management Report is prepared when it involves
replacement of worn-out spare parts.
c) Direct the General Services Officer to maintain records of the history of
repairs of each LGU property.

7.8 In view of the foregoing, we recommended that the LCE direct the persons
responsible to submit the lacking documents which led to the suspension of the
following transactions mentioned above in compliance with Section 5.4, 7.1.1 to
7.11.3 and 9.4 of COA Circular No. 2009-006 dated September 15, 2009.
Otherwise, Notice of Disallowance may be issued as prescribed by Section 9.4 of
the same circular.

5) In view of the foregoing, we recommend the strict compliance with the provisions of Sections 12 and 13 of Republic Act No. 9337, Section 2.57.2 (I) of Revenue Regulations No. 11-2018 dated January 31, 2018 and Section 4.114 of Revenue Regulations No. 02-98 dated April 17, 1998.
observations.managementAction: Implemented

observations.recommendation: 6) We recommend that Head of Agency must enforce the compliance of the above rules and regulations of COA Circular No. 2013004, dated January 30, 2013. 7) The Head of Agency shall inform its SA and ATL within (10) days after the award of the infrastructure project or before the start of the program/activity that the appropriate project signboards and/or public notices are already posted, and the SA and ATL shall validate the same.
observations.managementAction: Implemented

observations.recommendation: 8) We recommend that Head of Agency strictly adhere to the compliance of COA Circular No. 2009-001 dated February 12, 2009. Likewise, please submit justification why the agency failed to comply above rules and regulation.
observations.managementAction: Implemented

observations.recommendation: 11) We recommend to the Local Chief Executive to direct the municipal accountant to strictly enforce the submission of report and supporting documents to the auditor within the first ten (10) days of ensuing month, in compliance of Section 7 (7.2.1) of the Rules and Regulations on Settlement of Account (RRSA) and Sections 43 and 44 (k) of the Manual on the New Government Accounting System (MNGAS), for Local Government Units, Volume I.
observations.managementAction: Implemented

observations.recommendation: 17) We recommend Local Chief Executive to strictly comply with the limitations for the appropriation for discretionary funds provided in Sec. 325 (h) of RA 7160 and refer to Sec. 19 of the General Appropriations Act of 1989 as guide for its utilization.
observations.managementAction: Implemented

observations.recommendation: 18) Hence, we recommend that the Honorable Mayor strictly oversee the preparation and submission of the Local Disaster Risk Reduction and Management Fund Investment Plan (LDRRMFIP) by the Local Disaster Risk Reduction Officer based on the LDRRM Plan and Budget approved by the LDRRM Council. Furthermore, direct the Municipal Accountant, in coordination with the MDRRMO to prepare and submit the Report on the Utilization of DRRMF on a regular monthly basis which is on or before the 15th day after the end of each month, in compliance with the above quoted rules.
observations.managementAction: Implemented

observations.recommendation: 19) In view of the foregoing, we recommend the Local Chief Executive to direct and supervise the persons responsible to promptly submit the lacking documents which led to the suspension of the following transactions mentioned above in compliance with Section 7.1.1 of Commission on Audit (COA) Circular No. 2009-006, dated September 15, 2009. Otherwise, Notice of Disallowance may be issued as prescribed by Section 9.4 of the same circular.
observations.managementAction: Implemented

observations.recommendation: 10. Create an inventory committee of the municipality to conduct the physical count of its Road Network and prepare a report thereon; Require the Municipal Accountant to update the local Road Network account based on the result of the physical count and disclose the said account on the Notes to Financial Statements; Direct the General Services Officer to maintain a Local Road inventory and Road Map and prepare a report thereon at the end of every accounting period as well as keep a complete Local Road Network Property Card for all roads and its components; Require the Municipal Engineer to provide the accountant and the General Services Officer with the complete description and cost segregation of road components for road projects.
observations.managementAction: The management commits to follow your recommendations regarding this matter. We will create an inventory committee that will conduct the physical count of the Road Networks and other Property, Plant and Equipment. We will ensure to update the list of our PPEs and submit necessary reports.
observations.reason: Road Network not updated and inventory committee not created

observations.recommendation: 11. We recommend Local Chief Executive to strictly comply with the limitations for the appropriation of discretionary funds provided in Sec. 325 (h) of RA 7160 and refer to Sec. 44 of RA 11518 of the General Appropriations Act of 2021 as guide for its utilization.
observations.managementAction: The management commits to strictly comply the limitations for the appropriations for discretionary funds provided in Sec 325 (h) of RA 7160.
observations.reason: The management commits to implement this year.

observations.recommendation: 14. In view of the foregoing, we recommend the Local Chief Executive to direct and supervise the persons responsible to promptly submit the lacking documents which led to the suspension of the following transactions mentioned above in compliance with Section 7.1.1 of Commission on Audit (COA) Circular No. 2009-006, dated September 15, 2009. Otherwise, Notice of Disallowance may be issued as prescribed by Section 9.4 of the same circular.
observations.managementAction: The management commits to submit the lacking documents to settle the said suspensions.

observations.recommendation:
1.4 In relation to this, we recommend the Honorable Mayor to:
a) Create an inventory committee of the municipality to conduct the
physical count of its Road Network and prepare a report thereon;
b) Require the Municipal Accountant to update the local Road Network
account based on the result of the physical count and disclose the said
account on the Notes to Financial Statements;
c) Direct the General Services Officer to maintain a Local Road inventory
and Road Map and prepare a report thereon at the end of every
accounting period as well as keep a complete Local Road Network
Property Card for all roads and its components.
d) Require the Municipal Engineer to provide the accountant and the
General Services Officer with the complete description and cost
segregation of road components for road projects.

2.7 In view of the foregoing, we recommend the strict compliance with the
provisions of Sections 12 and 13 of Republic Act No. 9337, Section 2.57.2
(I) of Revenue Regulations No. 11-2018 dated January 31, 2018 and
Section 4.114 of Revenue Regulations No. 02-98 dated April 17, 1998.

3.5 We recommend to the Local Chief Executive to direct the municipal
accountant to strictly enforce the submission of report and supporting
documents to the auditor within the first ten (10) days of ensuing month,
in compliance of Section 7 (7.2.1) of the Rules and Regulations on
Settlement of Account (RRSA) and Sections 43 and 44 (k) of the Manual
on the New Government Accounting System (MNGAS), for Local
Government Units, Volume I.

4.5 We recommend that the Local Chief Executive shall:
a) Create an inventory committee to conduct the physical count of its
property, plant equipment which shall be made annually and reported
on the Report on the Physical Count of Property, Plant and Equipment
(RPCPPE), in accordance with Section 156 of COA Circular No. 92-
386 dated October 20, 1992; and
b) The reconciliation of the records of the Geneal Service Office and the
Municipal Accountant to up-date the property ledger card to reflect the
true and correct valuation of the said property and submit the same to
the Auditor concerned not later than January 31, of each year,
pursuant to Sections 119,120 and 124 of the Manual on the New
Government Accounting System (MNGAS) by Local Government
Units (LGUs) Volume I.

5.3 In view of the foregoing, we recommend the Local Chief Executive to
instruct the Accounting and Supply Management Office to coordinate in
the implementation of the Perpetual Inventory method of recording
Inventories. In addition to this, it should be ascertained that necessary
ledger cards are kept by the Accounting Office for each inventory, and
submission of the Report on the Physical Count of Inventory (RPCI) by
the Supply Management Office must be religiously complied with so the
former may make necessary actions or adjustments, if any.

6.7 We recommend Local Chief Executive to strictly comply with the
limitations for the appropriation of discretionary funds provided in Sec.
325 (h) of RA 7160 and refer to Sec. 44 of RA 11518 of the General
Appropriations Act of 2021 as guide for its utilization.

7.6 In line with this, we recommend the Local Chief Executive to:
a) Require the supply/property inspector to conduct pre-repair inspection
and post-repair inspection to the properties subjected to repairs and
furnish the reports to the COA Auditor within five (5) days from the
date of such evaluation/inspection;
b) See to it that a Waste Management Report is prepared when it involves
replacement of worn-out spare parts;
c) Direct the General Services Officer to maintain records of the history
of repairs of each LGU property; and

8.7 Hence, we recommend that the Honorable Mayor
a) Strictly oversee the preparation and submission of the Local Disaster
Risk Reduction and Management Fund Investment Plan (LDRRMFIP)
by the Local Disaster Risk Reduction Officer based on the LDRRM
Plan and Budget approved by the LDRRM Council.
b) Furthermore, direct the Municipal Accountant, in coordination with
the MDRRMO to prepare and submit the Report on the Utilization of
DRRMF on a regular monthly basis which is on or before the 15th day
after the end of each month, in compliance with the above quoted rules.

9.4 In view of the foregoing, we recommend the Local Chief Executive to
direct and supervise the persons responsible to promptly submit the
lacking documents which led to the suspension of the following
transactions mentioned above in compliance with Section 7.1.1 of
Commission on Audit (COA) Circular No. 2009-006, dated
September 15, 2009. Otherwise, Notice of Disallowance may be issued as
prescribed by Section 9.4 of the same circular.
1. That being said, we recommend the management to strictly comply with the provisions of Section 53 of R.A. No. 9184 in relation to the procedures of Negotiated Procurement.
observations.managementAction: A Strict compliance

observations.recommendation: 2. In view of the foregoing, we recommend the strict compliance with the provisions of Sections 12 and 13 of Republic Act No. 9337, Section 2.57.2 (I) of Revenue Regulations No. 11-2018 dated January 31, 2018 and Section 4.114 of Revenue Regulations No. 02-98 dated April 17, 1998.
observations.managementAction: The management commits to comply with the proper withholding of final and creditable taxes on Government Money Payments in compliance with Section 12 and 13 of RA No.9337.

observations.recommendation: 3.We recommend that the Head of Agency instruct the Accountant to prepare the Bank Reconciliation Statement (BRS) on a monthly basis in accordance with COA Circular 92-125-A dated March 4, 1992 and such BRS shall be submitted to the auditor within 15 days from the receipt of the bank statement, as stated in the same circular, for review and verification.
observations.managementAction: Bank Reconciliation Statement (BRS) was prepared in compliance with COA Circular 92-125-A dated March 4, 1992 and BRS shall be submitted to the auditor within 15 days from the receipt of the bank statement, as stated in the same circular, for review and verification.

observations.recommendation: 4. We recommend the Local Chief Executive to direct the municipal accountant to strictly enforce the submission of Report of Checks Issued (RCI) and Report of Disbursements (RD) for the Calendar Year 2021, in violation of Section 7 (7.2.1) of the Rules and Regulations on Settlement of Account (RRSA) and Sections 43 and 44 (k) of the Manual on the New Government Accounting System (MNGAS), for Local Government Units, Volume I.
observations.managementAction: Submission of Report of Checks Issued (RCI) and Report of Disbursements (RD) were complied
observations.reason: Reports were complied but delayed.

observations.recommendation: 5. Thus, we recommend the Local Chief Executive to direct the Accountant/Treasurer to strictly comply with the provisions of the above stated circulars to maintain a sound internal control in the disbursement of government funds.
observations.managementAction: Disbursement Vouchers were able to submit the Disbursement Vouchers and related reports. Compliance to the rules on a timely submission of reports were delayed.

observations.recommendation: 6. We recommend that Head of Agency must enforce the compliance of the above rules and regulations of COA Circular No. 2013004, dated January 30, 2013.
observations.managementAction: Finished projects for CY 2018 were submitted for review. Likewise, please submit justification why the agency were not able to submit the ongoing projects 2021 for technical review but requested for technical review and inspection.
observations.reason: Ongoing projects 2021 were not submitted

observations.recommendation: 7. We recommend that Head of Agency strictly adhere to the compliance of COA Circular No. 2009001 dated February 12, 2009.
observations.managementAction: The projects for CY 2018 were submitted for contract/ technical review and inspection. The projects and supporting documents were submitted but ongoing projects 2021 were not submitted.
observations.reason: Ongoing projects 2021 were not submitted

observations.recommendation: 8. We recommend that the Local Chief Executive: a) Create an inventory committee to conduct the physical count of its property, plant equipment which shall be made annually and reported on the Report on the Physical Count of Property, Plant and Equipment (RPCPPE), in accordance with Section 156 of COA Circular No. 92-386 dated October 20, 1992; and b) The reconciliation of the records of the General Service Office and the Municipal Accountant to up-date the property ledger card to reflect the true and correct valuation of the said property and submit the same to the Auditor concerned not later than January 31, of each year, pursuant to Sections 119,120 and 124 of the Manual on the New Government Accounting System (MNGAS) by Local Government Units (LGUs) Volume I.
observations.managementAction: The management is amenable of the findings and commits to abide with the audit recommendation to create an inventory committee to conduct inventory of Property Plants and Equipment.
observations.reason: Property management and inventory committee not implemented

observations.recommendation: 9. In view of the foregoing, we recommend the Local Chief Executive to direct the municipal accountant to strictly enforce the submission of report and supporting documents to the auditor within the first ten (10) days of ensuing month, in compliance of Section 7 (7.2.1) of the Rules and Regulations on Settlement of Account (RRSA) and Sections 43 and 44 (k) of the Manual on the New Government Accounting System (MNGAS), for Local Government Units, Volume I.
observations.managementAction: Accountant were able to submit the Disbursement Vouchers and related reports. Compliance to the rules on a timely submission of reports were delayed.
observations.reason: Disbursement Vouchers and related reports were submitted after 1 year delayed.

observations.recommendation: 12. We recommended that management to direct the accountant to: a) prepare adjusting entries to correct overstatement of account Subsidy to Other LGU, b) set-up the account Receivable from the respective LGU which are subject for liquidation, in compliance with COA Circular 2015-009.
observations.managementAction: Related accounts were adjusted and corrected.

observations.recommendation: 13. Hence, we recommend that the Honorable Mayor a) Strictly oversee the preparation and submission of the Local Disaster Risk Reduction and Management Fund Investment Plan (LDRRMFIP) by the Local Disaster Risk Reduction Officer based on the LDRRM Plan and Budget approved by the LDRRM Council. b) Furthermore, direct the Municipal Accountant, in coordination with the MDRRMO to prepare and submit the Report on the Utilization of DRRMF on a regular monthly basis which is on or before the 15th day after the end of each month, in compliance with the above quoted rules.
observations.managementAction: Local Disaster Risk Officer commits to submit the Local Disaster Risk Reduction and Management Fund Investment Plan (LDRRMFIP).
observations.reason: Report on the Utilization of DRRMF were submitted but LDRRMFIP is still to be submitted

observations.recommendation: We recommend that the Local Chief Executive requires: a. The General Services Officer to create an inventory committee to conduct the physical count of its properties, plant equipment, and shall be made annually and reported on the Report on the Physical Count of Property, Plant and Equipment (RPCPPE). b. The reconciliation of the record of the GSO and the Municipal Accountant to update the property ledger card to reflect the true and correct valuation of the said property and submit the same to the Auditor concerned not later than January 31, of each year.
observations.managementAction: The Office of the Accountant was directed to comply the required Partially implemented.
observations.reason: Failure to create an inventory committee. Stock and ledger card were not updated and reconciled; physical count not conducted.

observations.recommendation: In relation to this, we recommend the Honorable Mayor to: a. Create an inventory committee of the municipality to conduct the physical count of its Road Network and prepare a report thereon (see Annex C); b. Require the Municipal Accountant to update the local Road Network account based on the result of the physical count and disclose the said account on the Notes to Financial Statements (see Annex B); c. Direct the General Services Officer to maintain a Local Road inventory and Road Map and prepare a report thereon at the end of every accounting period (See Annex A) as well as keep a complete Local Road Network Property Card for all roads and its components (see Annex E). d. Require the Municipal Engineer to provide the accountant and the General Services Officer with the complete description and cost segregation of road components for road projects.
observations.managementAction: The management commits to follow our recommendations regarding this matter. They will create an inventory committee that will conduct the physical count of the Road Networks and other Property, Plant and Equipment. They will ensure to update the list of their PPEs and submit necessary reports.
observations.reason: Failure to create an inventory committee; lack of submission of required inventory and reconciliation.

observations.recommendation: A LDRRMFIP for the DRRM program shall be prepared annually. It shall present the 30% allocation for QRF in lump-sum and the allocation for disaster mitigation, prevention and preparedness with details as to projects and activities to be funded.
observations.managementAction: Management to prepare and submit required plans and monthly reports.
observations.reason: Non-preparation and non-submission of the Annual LDRRMFIP; non-submission of detailed monthly reports precluded proper monitoring.

observations.recommendation: We recommend that the Head of Agency instruct the Accountant to prepare the Bank Reconciliation Statement (BRS) on a monthly basis in accordance with COA Circular 92-125-A dated March 4, 1992 and such BRS shall be submitted to the auditor within 15 days from the receipt of the bank statement, as stated in the same circular, for review and verification.
observations.managementAction: The management commits to submit the Bank Reconciliation Statement for the year 2020 as soon as possible and faithfully comply with the prescribed time for submission of the BRS which is 15 days after receipt of the Bank Statement.
observations.reason: Delay of Bank Statements and failure to prepare/submit BRS monthly.

observations.recommendation:
That being said, we recommend the management to strictly comply
with the provisions of Section 53 of R.A. No. 9184 in relation to the
procedures of Negotiated Procurement.

In view of the foregoing, we recommend the strict compliance with
the provisions of Sections 12 and 13 of Republic Act No. 9337, Section 2.57.2
(I) of Revenue Regulations No. 11-2018 dated January 31, 2018 and Section
4.114 of Revenue Regulations No. 02-98 dated April 17, 1998.

Thus, we recommend that the Local Chief Executive to direct the
Municipal Accountant/Treasurer to strictly comply with the provisions of the
above stated circulars to maintain a sound internal control in the
disbursement of government funds.

In relation to this, we recommend the Honorable Mayor to:
a. Create an inventory committee of the municipality to conduct the
physical count of its Road Network and prepare a report thereon (see
Annex C);
b. Require the Municipal Accountant to update the local Road Network
account based on the result of the physical count and disclose the said
account on the Notes to Financial Statements (see Annex B);
c. Direct the General Services Officer to maintain a Local Road inventory
and Road Map and prepare a report thereon at the end of every
accounting period (See Annex A) as well as keep a complete Local Road
Network Property Card for all roads and its components (see Annex E).
d. Require the Municipal Engineer to provide the accountant and the
General Services Officer with the complete description and cost
segregation of road components for road projects.

We recommend Local Chief Executive to direct the municipal
accountant to strictly enforce the submission of Report of Checks Issued
(RCI) and Report of Disbursements (RD) to the auditor within first ten (10)
days of ensuing month in compliance of Section 7 (7.2.1) of the Rules and
Regulations on Settlement of Account RRSA and LGU NGAS Vol. I.

We recommend that the Local Chief Executive to require:
a. The General Services Officer (GSO) to create an inventory committee to
conduct the physical count of its properties, plant equipment which shall
be made annually and reported on the Report on the Physical Count of
Property, Plant and Equipment (RPCPPE).
b. The reconciliation of the record of the GSO and the Municipal
Accountant to update the property ledger card to reflect the true and
correct valuation of the said property and submit the same to the Auditor
concerned not later than January 31, of each year.

We recommend Local Chief Executive to direct the municipal accountant to
strictly enforce the submission of report and supporting documents to the auditor
within first ten (10) days of ensuing month in compliance of Section 7 (7.2.1) of
the Rules and Regulations on Settlement of Account and Sections 43 and 44 of the
Manual on the New Government Accounting System (MNGAS), for Local
Government Units, Volume I.

We recommend that the Head of Agency instruct the Accountant to
prepare the Bank Reconciliation Statement (BRS) on a monthly basis in
accordance with COA Circular 92-125-A dated March 4, 1992 and such BRS
shall be submitted to the auditor within 15 days from the receipt of the bank
statement, as stated in the same circular, for review and verification.

We recommend to the Local Chief Executive (LCE) to instruct the personnel
concerned to settle their Notice of Suspension, pursuant to Section 9.4 of the
Commission on Audit (COA) Circular No. 2009-006 dated September 15, 2009. And
strictly follow the rules and regulations on the disbursement of funds with complete
documentation to avoid suspension in audit in accordance with Section 4(6) of the
Presidential Decree (P.D.) No. 1445, otherwise known as the Government Auditing
Code of the Philippines.

Hence, we recommend that the Honorable Mayor strictly oversee the
preparation and submission of the Local Disaster Risk Reduction and Management
Fund Investment Plan (LDRRMFIP) by the Local Disaster Risk Reduction Officer
based on the LDRRM Plan and Budget approved by the LDRRM Council.
Furthermore, direct the Municipal Accountant, in coordination with the
MDRRMO to prepare and submit the Report on the Utilization of DRRMF on a
regular monthly basis which is on or before the 15th day after the end of each
month, in compliance with the above quoted rules.
We recommend that Head of Agency must enforce the compliance of the above rules and regulations of COA government Circular No. 2013004, dated January 30, 2013.
observations.managementAction: A manpower was committed to comply with the recommendation. The Head of Agency shall inform its SA and ATL within (10) days after the award of the infrastructure project or before the start of the program/activity that the appropriate project signboards and/or public notices are already posted, and the SA and ATL shall validate the same.
observations.reason: Lack of manpower but management was committed to comply with the recommendation.

observations.recommendation: We recommend that Head of Agency strictly adhere to the compliance of COA Circular No. 2009-001 dated February 12, 2009. Likewise, please submit justification why the agency failed to comply above rules and regulation.
observations.managementAction: The projects for CY 2018 were Partially submitted and requested for justification why the agency failed to comply above rules and regulation.
observations.reason: Requested for justification why the agency failed to comply; technical regulation on submission and issuance thereof were not complied fully.

observations.recommendation: We recommend that Local Chief Executive to strictly enforce the submission of Report of Checks Issued (RCI) and Report of Disbursements (RD) to the auditor within first ten (10) days of ensuing month in compliance of Section 7 (7.2.1) of the Rules and Regulations on Settlement of Account and Sections 43 and 44(k) of the Manual on the New Government Accounting System (MNGAS) for Local Government Units (LGUs), Volume I.
observations.managementAction: The management commits to submit Report of Checks Issued (RCI) and Report of Disbursements (RD) for the calendar year 2020. And as for the subsequent reports, the management commits to submit them at the earliest possible time.
observations.reason: The management submitted some required reports but not all; issues due to lack of personnel and COVID related reasons.

observations.recommendation: We recommend Local Chief Executive to direct the municipal accountant to strictly enforce the submission of report and supporting documents to the auditor within first ten (10) days of ensuing month in compliance of Section 7 (7.2.1) of the Rules and Regulations on Settlement of Account and Sections 43 and 44(k) of the Manual on the New Government Accounting System (MNGAS), for Local Government Units, Volume I.
observations.managementAction: The management commits to submit the above-stated documents as soon as possible as well as the compliance with the rules on submission of paid disbursement vouchers and other related reports pertaining to the disbursement of all funds.
observations.reason: Though already submitted, the indicated reports still remained not complied with, thus the reason for partial implementation.

observations.recommendation: Hence, we recommend that the Honorable Mayor strictly oversee the preparation and submission of the Local Disaster Risk Reduction and Management Fund Investment Plan (LDRRMFIP) by the Local Disaster Risk Reduction Officer based on the LDRRM Plan and Budget approved by the LDRRM Council. Furthermore, direct the Municipal Accountant, in coordination with the MDRRMO to prepare and submit the Report on the Utilization of DRRMF on a regular monthly basis which is on or before the 15th day after the end of each month, in compliance with the above quoted rules.
observations.managementAction: The management commits to submit to the auditor the Local Disaster Risk Reduction and Management Fund Investment Plan (LDRRMFIP) as well as the monthly report on utilization in compliance with COA Circular 2012-002.
observations.reason: Quarterly reports were submitted but the Management remained unaware of the required format; monthly utilization reports not submitted.

observations.recommendation: We recommended that management direct the Accountant to: a. effect the necessary adjustments/corrections to correct the misstatements in the Payable account and henceforth, ensure that JEVs are properly prepared and reviewed prior to recording. b. adopt the effective monitoring scheme on the submission of liquidation reports and immediate liquidation of outstanding fund transfers.

observations.recommendation:
Recommendation
In relation to this, we recommend the Honorable Mayor:
a. Abide Section V.1, V.2, V.3, V.8 and V.9 of Commission On Audit Circular 2015-008 dated November 23, 2015.
b. Create an inventory committee of the municipality to conduct the physical count of its Road Network and prepare a report thereon (see Annex C);
c. Require the Municipal Accountant to update the local Road Network account based on the result of the physical count and disclose the said account on the Notes to Financial Statements (see Annex B);
d. Direct the General Services Officer to maintain a Local Road inventory and Road Map and prepare a report thereon at the end of every accounting period (See Annex A) as well as keep a complete Local Road Network Property Card for all roads and its components (see Annex E).
e. Require the Municipal Engineer to provide the accountant and the General Services Officer with the complete description and cost segregation of road components for road projects.

Recommendation
We recommend that the Local Chief Executive requires:
a. The General Services Officer to create an inventory committee to conduct the physical count of its properties, plant equipment, and shall be made annually and reported on the Report on the Physical Count of Property, Plant and Equipment (RPCPPE), in accordance with Section 156 of the Commission on Audit (COA) Circular No. 92-386 dated October 20, 1992.
b. The reconciliation of the record of the GSO and the Municipal Accountant to up-date the property ledger card to reflect the true and correct valuation of the said property and submit the same to the Auditor concerned not later than January 31, of each year, pursuant to Sections 119, 120 and 124 of the Manual New Government Accounting System (MNGAS) by Local Government Unit, Volume I.

Recommendation
We recommend Local Chief Executive to strictly comply with the limitations for the appropriation for discretionary funds provided in Sec. 325 (h) of RA 7160 and refer to Sec. 19 of the General Appropriations Act of 1989 as guide for its utilization.

Recommendation
We recommend Local Chief Executive to direct the municipal accountant to strictly enforce the submission of report and supporting documents to the auditor within first ten (10) days of ensuing month in compliance of Section 7 (7.2.1) of the Rules and Regulations on Settlement of Account RRSA andLGU NGAS Vol. I.

Recommendation
Hence, we recommend that the Honorable Mayor strictly oversee the preparation and submission of the Local Disaster Risk Reduction and Management Fund Investment Plan (LDRRMFIP) by the Local Disaster Risk Reduction Officer based on the LDRRM Plan and Budget approved by the LDRRM Council.
Furthermore, direct the Municipal Accountant, in coordination with the MDRRMO to prepare and submit the Report on the Utilization of DRRMF on a regular monthly basis which is on or before the 15th day after the end of each month, in compliance with the above quoted rules.

Recommendation
We recommend to the Local Chief Executive (LCE) to instruct the personnel concerned to settle their respective Notice of Suspension, pursuant to Section 9.4 of the Commission on Audit (COA) Circular No. 2009-006 dated September 15, 2009. And strictly follow the rules and regulations on the disbursement of funds with complete documentation to avoid suspension in audit.
We recommend that the Municipal Accountant to comply immediately submit the required supporting documents. Stop the practice of paying transactions without complete supporting documents.
observations.managementAction: Accountant was directed to comply the required supporting documents. Notice of Suspensions were issued on some transactions.
observations.reason: The Office of the Municipal Accountant is lacking of personnel. Notice of Suspensions were issued on some transactions.

The personnel concerned to ensure that the unimplemented AIPs are completely implemented in the succeeding year.

observations.recommendation:
We recommended that management direct the Accountant to:
a. Effect the necessary adjustments/corrections to correct the
misstatements in the Payable account and henceforth, ensure that JEVs
are properly prepared and reviewed prior to recording.
b. Adopt the effective monitoring scheme on the submission of liquidation
reports and immediate liquidation of outstanding fund transfers.
c. Submit disbursement vouchers with supporting documents of
transactions of trust fund.

We recommended that management direct the Accountant to:
a. transfer funds to Other LGU for any financial assistant in compliance
with COA Circular 2015-009.
b. direct the accountant to set-up the Receivable account from LGU which
are subject for liquidation or outright record as Subsidy to Other Local
Government Unit.
c. prepare adjusting entries to correct overstatement of account Subsidy to
Other LGU.

We recommend that Head of Agency must enforce the compliance of the
above rules and regulations of COA Circular No. 2013-004, dated January 30, 2013.
The Head of Agency shall inform its SA and ATL within (10) days after the
award of the infrastructure project or before the start of the program/activity that the
appropriate project signboards and/or public notices are already posted, and the SA
and ATL shall validate the same.

We recommend that Head of Agency strictly adhere to the compliance of
COA Circular No. 2009-001 dated February 12, 2009. Likewise, please submit
justification why the agency failed to comply above rules and regulation.

We recommend Local Chief Executive to direct the municipal accountant to
strictly enforce the submission of report and supporting documents to the auditor
within first ten (10) days of ensuing month in compliance of Section 7 (7.2.1) of the
Rules and Regulations on Settlement of Account RRSA and LGU NGAS Vol. I.

We recommend that the Local Chief Executive requires:
b. The General Services Officer to create an inventory committee to
conduct the physical count of its properties, plant equipment, and
shall be made annually and reported on the Report on the Physical
Count of Property, Plant and Equipment (RPCPPE).
c. The reconciliation of the record of the GSO and the Municipal
Accountant to up-date the property ledger card to reflect the true and
correct valuation of the said property and submit the same to the
Auditor concerned not later than January 31, of each year.

We recommend that the LCE direct:
a. Direct the Municipal Engineer to furnish a copy of the Contracts
and Purchase Orders together with the supporting documents of
procurement related to the implementation of
Programs/Projects/Activities of 20% Development Fund for our
validation and inspection of Projects.
b. The personnel concerned to ensure that the unimplemented AIPs
are completely implemented in the succeeding year.

We recommend that the Head of Agency shall instruct the Accountant to prepare the Bank Reconciliation Statement (BRS) on a monthly basis in accordance with the COA Circular 92-125-A dated March 4, 1992. Such BRS shall be regularly submitted to the auditor for review and verification.

We recommend that the Local Chief Executive requires:
the General Services Officer to create an inventory committee to conduct the physical count of its properties, plant equipment, and shall be made annually and reported on the Report on the Physical Count of Property, Plant and Equipment (RPCPPE).
The reconciliation of the record of the GSO and the Municipal Accountant to up-date the property ledger card to reflect the true and correct valuation of the said property and submit the same to the Auditor concerned not later than January 31, of each year.

We recommend that the Head of Agency shall instruct the Accountant to prepare the Bank Reconciliation Statement (BRS) on a monthly basis in accordance with COA Circular 92-125-A dated March 4, 1992.  Such BRS shall be regularly submitted to the auditor for review and verification.

We recommend that the Municipal Mayor direct the Municipal Accountant to immediately submit the lacking supporting documents. Stop the practice of paying transactions without complete supporting documents.
This is without prejudice to the issuance of the Notice of Suspension and Disallowances if warranted.
We recommend that Head of Agency to strictly enforce the compliance of Section 7 (7.2.1) of the Rules and Regulations on Settlement of Account RRSA and Section 179 of GAAM
We recommend that Head of Agency shall strictly enforce the compliance of Section 7 (7.2.1) of RRSA, Section 43 and 44 of LGU NGAS Vol. I.

We recommend that the LCE direct:
The local budget council to meticulously plan the procurement of the PPA’s funded out of 20% Development Fund and to consider the important development projects in their budget proposal to insure its implementation in accordance with the provisions of JMC 2017-1.
The personnel concerned to ensure that the unimplemented AIPs are completely implemented in the succeeding year.

We recommended the Local Chief Executive to direct the Project Engineer to submit a copy of the Contract and related supporting documents to the Office of the Auditor for review of contract and proper monitoring of implementation of project.

We recommend to the Local Chief Executive (LCE) to instruct the personnel concerned to settle their Notice of Suspension, pursuant to Section 9.4 of the Commission on Audit (COA) Circular No. 2009-006 September 15, 2009.  And strictly follow the rules and regulations on the disbursement of funds with complete documentation to avoid suspension in audit.

We recommend that the LCE direct the LDRRFO and Municipal Accountant to submit the Local Disaster Risk Reduction Fund Project (LDRRMFP) and the monthly report of Source and Utilization of DRRMF and strictly observe the disclosure requirements of Unexpended balance of Quick Response Fund (QRF) and the MOOE of LDRRMF in the Notes to Financial Statement.
We recommend that Head of Agency to strictly enforce the compliance of Section 7 (7.2.1) of the Rules and Regulations on Settlement of Account RRSA and Section 179 of GAAM.

And strictly follow the rules and regulations on the disbursement of funds with complete documentation to avoid suspension in audit.
